name: Las Salinas de Poza Trail
name_original: Ruta Salinas de Poza — circular por el Castillo de los Rojas
description: Hike the moderate 4.1-mile Las Salinas de Poza Trail to explore ancient salt flats, unique geology, and views of Poza de la Sal castle.
region_intro: Hiking around Poza De La Sal offers exploration of a landscape shaped by unique geological features and historical salt flats. The region is characterized by the ancient salt diapir, a significant geological structure, alongside varied terrain that includes pine and oak forests. Trails provide expansive views of the surrounding La Bureba region, the Obarenes Mountains, and the Sierra del Alto de las Cruces. The area combines natural beauty with historical elements, such as the medieval town and its castle. Best hiking trails around Poza De La Sal The most popular hiking route is Las Salinas de Poza Trail, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours to complete. This moderate route explores the ancient salt flats and offers views of Poza de la Sal castle. Another top favourite among local hikers is Town Hall Arch – Poza de la Sal Saltworks loop from Poza de la Sal, a moderate 6.2 miles (9.9 km) path. This circular route traverses the saltworks and surrounding varied terrain. Local hikers also love the San Mamés Mártir Church – CascadaDeLaHuevera loop from Padrones de Bureba, a 6.9 miles (11.0 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Poza De La Sal is defined by ancient salt flats, geological diapirs, and mixed forests of pine and oak. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Poza De La Sal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Poza De La Sal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Poza de la Sal?
A: There are nearly 40 hiking trails around Poza de la Sal, offering a diverse range of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners in Poza de la Sal?
A: Yes, Poza de la Sal offers several easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24637555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall Arch – FuenteBuena loop from Poza de la Sal&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that can be enjoyed by those looking for a less strenuous walk, traversing varied terrain without significant elevation gain.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many trails around Poza de la Sal are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14991163" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Town Hall Arch – Poza de la Sal Saltworks loop from Poza de la Sal&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the historic saltworks and surrounding landscapes.

Q: What are the main natural attractions to see while hiking in Poza de la Sal?
A: The primary natural attraction is the unique geological formation of the ancient Salt Flats of Poza de la Sal, including its impressive salt diapir. Hikers can also explore varied landscapes with pine and oak forests, and enjoy panoramic views from viewpoints like El Picón de Santa Engracia. You might also encounter natural monuments such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4405981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Red Rock Valley&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5295093" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elephant Rock&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to visit on the hiking trails?
A: Yes, one notable natural feature is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6676863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;CascadaDeLaHuevera&lt;/a&gt;. You can explore this area on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Mamés Mártir Church – CascadaDeLaHuevera loop from Padrones de Bureba&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through diverse landscapes to this natural monument.

Q: What historical landmarks can be explored on hikes around Poza de la Sal?
A: Poza de la Sal is rich in history. Hikers can visit the 14th-15th century Castle of Los Rojas, which once defended the salt pans, and explore the medieval town itself. The ancient salt flats are also a historical landmark, with exploitation dating back to Roman times. Nearby, you can also find historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6365773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oña Historic Center&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options available?
A: While many routes are day hikes, the region offers options for longer excursions. For example, the 'Poza - Castil - Abajas - Páramo - Poza' route is a 24 km trail, described as easy in difficulty but medium in toughness, featuring varied terrain and notable inclines.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Poza de la Sal?
A: The trails in Poza de la Sal are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique geological features, such as the salt flats and diapir, the diverse scenery including forests and panoramic views, and the blend of natural beauty with historical elements.

Q: Can I hike through the famous salt flats of Poza de la Sal?
A: Yes, exploring the ancient salt flats is a highlight of hiking in the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1047060098/las-salinas-de-poza-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Salinas de Poza Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.1-mile route specifically designed to allow you to explore this unique geological formation and learn about its traditional salt extraction process.

Q: Are there routes that offer panoramic views of the region?
A: Many trails provide stunning panoramic views. Routes that ascend through pine and oak forests often culminate in expansive vistas of the La Bureba region, the Obarenes Mountains, and the Sierra del Alto de las Cruces. The El Picón de Santa Engracia Viewpoint and the Castle of Los Rojas are excellent spots for breathtaking scenery.

Q: Are there any routes that follow old railway lines?
A: Yes, a simpler route known as the Vía Verde follows old railway lines that have been converted into a greenway. This offers a relatively flat and accessible path for walking or cycling, providing a different perspective of the landscape.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking?
A: You can expect a diverse range of landscapes, from the unique geological formations of the salt flats and diapir to closed paths, and mixed forests of pine and oak. Many routes also feature significant ascents that open up to expansive views of the surrounding regions.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Las Salinas de Poza Trail?
faqs.answer: The Las Salinas de Poza Trail is generally rated as moderate. While some parts are accessible with little slope, the common circular route of about 6.57 km (4 miles) includes an elevation gain of 230 meters (755 feet) and an initial strong ascent towards the center of the diapir. Good fitness is recommended for this hike, which typically takes around 2 hours to complete.

faqs.question: What unique geological features can I see on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is set within the extraordinary Diapiro de Poza de la Sal, a 2.5 km diameter geological formation. You'll encounter varied terrain, including dark, hard rock outcrops called ophites with unique castellated shapes, formed by the upward intrusion of salt, marl, clay, and gypsum over millions of years.

faqs.question: What historical points of interest are along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail is rich in history! You can explore the ancient salt flats, which have been exploited since prehistoric or Roman times. Look out for preserved infrastructure like crystallization platforms (eras), wells, and the remains of old warehouses. You'll also get views of the medieval town of Poza de la Sal and its imposing Castle of Poza de la Sal. Other highlights include the Church of San Cosme and San Damian and the Palace of the Marquises of Poza.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is located in Poza de la Sal, Burgos, Spain. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's generally accessible from the village. Given its popularity, parking is typically available near the main access points or within the village itself. It's always a good idea to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the Las Salinas de Poza Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular trail are not detailed, many trails in Spain allow dogs, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's advisable to keep your dog leashed to protect local wildlife and for the safety of other hikers, especially given the varied terrain and potential for historical structures.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to hike the Las Salinas de Poza Trail. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike the Las Salinas de Poza Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, so an early start is recommended if hiking during those months. Winter conditions might include colder temperatures, but the trail is generally accessible.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail offers several panoramic views. You'll find excellent vistas from the Views from the Castillo de los Rojas, overlooking the medieval town of Poza de la Sal, the Montes Obarenes, the flat expanse of La Bureba, the Sierra de la Demanda, and the circular valley of "El Salero."

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Las Salinas de Poza Trail (also known as SL-BU 67 or PRC-BU 67) is part of a network of paths. The route itself covers 99% of the official "Las Salinas de Poza" trail and also passes through sections of the "[CIMA BU06] Altotero ~ BU-V-5021" and "[CIMA BU06] Altotero ~ CL-633" trails.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or places to eat near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near the charming medieval village of Poza de la Sal. You'll likely find local cafes, restaurants, and possibly accommodation options within the village to refresh yourself before or after your hike. An interpretation center in the Casa de Administración de las Reales Salinas also provides further context about the saltworks.

faqs.question: Is the Las Salinas de Poza Trail a loop or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: The most common and recommended version of the Las Salinas de Poza Trail is a circular route. This allows you to experience the diverse geological formations and historical sites without retracing your steps, offering a comprehensive exploration of the area.
